title: Redential proof bundle v1
description: Metadata-only proof of git activity. This schema IS the trust contract: nothing outside it ever leaves the user's machine.

repo_fingerprint.description: Salted hash of the root commit sha. Lets the server detect re-submissions of the same repo without knowing which repo it is.

author_identity_hashes.description: Salted hashes of the author emails the USER explicitly selected as their own. Never plaintext, never anyone else's.

hour_histogram.description: Commits per hour-of-day (UTC). Behavioral fingerprint anchor.

categories.description: Technical categories inferred LOCALLY from paths/extensions before hashing. The category survives; the path does not.

detected_skills.description: Skills detected LOCALLY by deterministic signature matching (signatures/*.json) over diff contents. Zero network, no LLMs. Closed vocabulary: every slug MUST exist in this repo's public taxonomy.json — a slug outside that list makes the bundle invalid (enforced by the CLI validator on top of this schema). May be empty.

slug.description: Skill slug from taxonomy.json, e.g. "payments/stripe". The pattern bounds the shape; membership in taxonomy.json is validated separately and is mandatory.

evidence.description: Optional. Detection tier for this entry: "structural" means the slug was verified by following connected code relations (anchors + call-graph reachability), not just a single matched import line. "import" is reserved for a future explicit-tagging change; in schema 1.2.0 the field is only ever emitted for "structural" entries — an absent field means an ordinary import-tier match, same as every 1.1.0 bundle. See docs/schema.md.

confidence.description: Optional, only present alongside evidence:"structural". "direct" means the connected shape was found in the same function or file; "inferred" means it was found across files within a bounded search. AMBIGUOUS classifications are never emitted under this or any other field — ambiguous means the skill is not claimed in the bundle at all, full stop. See docs/schema.md.

agent_config_present.description: Which agent tooling signals were detected (e.g. CLAUDE.md present). Booleans by tool family, never file contents.

merkle_root.description: Merkle root over the user's commit shas. Enables future re-verification and consistency checks without revealing the shas.

date_forensics.description: Aggregate rewrite-forensics signal from git's two independent commit dates (author vs. committer). A HEURISTIC signal for server-side scoring only — never a local pass/fail verdict. See docs/schema.md for the measurement contract and expected ranges.

mismatch_ratio.description: Fraction of the user's commits whose committer date differs from its author date by more than 48h.

committer_burst_ratio.description: Fraction of the user's commits whose committer date falls inside the single densest 24h window of committer dates.

authorized_confirmation.description: The user explicitly confirmed: 'I am authorized to analyze this repository.'
